title: Creamy Lemon Pepper Parmesan Chicken
instructions: Season chicken thighs evenly on both sides with 1/2 teaspoon salt and 1/2 teaspoon black pepper.
Heat olive oil in a 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat. Add chicken thighs and cook until browned, turning once, 7 to 8 minutes total. Remove chicken from skillet and set aside.
Add shallot and garlic to the same skillet. Cook, stirring, until fragrant, 1 to 2 minutes. Pour in chicken broth and heavy cream. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Stir in Parmesan cheese, lemon zest, remaining 1 teaspoon black pepper, and remaining 1/4 teaspoon salt. Simmer until the sauce thickens slightly, 4 to 5 minutes.
Return chicken to the skillet and cook until heated through, a thermometer inserted into the thickest portion registers 165 degrees F (74 degrees C), and chicken is coated with sauce, 2 to 3 minutes.
Garnish with parsley and serve with lemon wedges. Recipe developed by Jaelyn Luong
